focus: RQ2: presentation ownership, bounded inline exceptions, and owner-label correctness across six command families
noveltyJustification: 3 of 5 findings were fully new, 2 refined known defects, and the typed owner/exception model adds a 0.10 simplicity bonus by resolving RQ2.
ruledOut: Hand-authoring file-type labels beside authoritative asset paths

title: Presentation authority is topology-independent
summary: All six families point to one presentation asset while routers and workflow mechanisms retain routing/execution responsibilities.

title: Presentation Boundary is a capability fence
summary: The boundary enumerates asset-owned visible surfaces and load-before-render obligations; it does not create another owner.

title: memory/search is a bounded copied-invariant exception
summary: The router repeats only the compressed retrieval envelope as a hard reminder while the presentation asset remains authoritative for all display surfaces.

title: Typed exceptions should preserve asset ownership
summary: Model one asset owner plus optional bounded inline copies with kind, anchors, surfaces, and rationale; do not model router-exception as a competing owner.

title: Mislabeled owners are prose media-type drift
summary: Routers reference .txt presentation contracts but call them presentation Markdown; generated neutral labels remove the duplicate fact.

acceptanceCriterion: Every command resolves one existing presentation owner; inline render content fails unless a bounded exception declares rationale, stable router/source anchors, and surfaces.
Owned Assets and Presentation Boundary are generated from typed metadata and contain no hand-authored media-type label.
Only the anchored retrieval hard-reminder block is allowed inline; widened or unpaired copies fail validation.
No current router says presentation Markdown for a text/plain presentation contract, and regeneration cannot reintroduce the mismatch.
